'''Xerton Nine''' was an astronomical object that occupied the ninth [[Orbit/Legends|orbital]] position<ref name="Blasters" /> within the [[Xerton system/Legends|Xerton system]] of [[Wild Space/Legends|Wild Space]]. The [[Braxant Run/Legends|Braxant Run]] [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlane]] linked the Xerton [[Star system/Legends|system]] to the [[Qonto system/Legends|Qonto system]].<ref name="TEA" /> At some point following the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]]<ref name="Blasters" /> that occurred in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]],<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> the [[Grand Magnus]] was a title held by an individual from Xerton Nine.<ref name="Blasters" />

In a [[Scandoc/Legends|scandoc]] outlining [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]] tactics that was released around that [[Time/Legends|time]], the former [[New Republic Defense Force/Legends|New Republic military]] [[Colonel/Legends|colonel]] [[Andrephan Stormcaller]] mentioned that a mercenary using proper [[Forger (profession)/Legends|forged]] documentation, especially one produced by an [[ID processor]], could impersonate anyone, from an [[Customs Inspector|Imperial customs inspector]] to the Grand Magnus from Xerton Nine.<ref name="Blasters" />

==Behind the scenes==
Xerton Nine was first mentioned in "[[Blasters for Hire]]," a [[1994]] ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'' source article written by [[Tony Russo|Anthony P. Russo]] and published in [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 3|''Star Wars Adventure Journal'' 3]].<ref name="Blasters" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Xerton system, and therefore Xerton Nine, in grid square J-3.<ref name="TEA" />
